question: Most abundant cells in Cell rich zone

Agent1 Round 2: Given my focus here has been strictly aligned with dental and oral healthcare topics, I recognize there may be some overlap but limited relevance regarding the precise nature of each cell type listed—fibroblasts being more common in connective tissues rather than enamel-forming areas; odontoblasts involved directly in dentin production; ameloblasts specialized for enamel formation, yet typically less numerous compared to others mentioned especially outside active tooth development phases; undifferentiated mesenchymal stem cells present potentially across many sites given their multipotency—but still largely localized based on context clues about "cell rich zones."
